Dude, "It doesn't clarify that the ordering problem is about event streaming/transport, not Event Sourcing itself", read also again @qa-article.md with the discussion i had with Peter that triggered this discussion. It clearly states that's about messages coming from messaging system, e.g. RabbitMQ and getting out of order because of race conditions between quueues and modules. Then if we store them as they go, we'll be reading them in the order as they were appended to our local event store, not in order they were created. And it's not even inherited to event sourcing, if we didn't have event store and event sourcing but just updating read model then we'd have the same issue. So the article is about explaing this issue, and how to fix it using read models to filter out the noise, transform, and localise information from the external world. Also keeping in mind that we should revisit if we shouldn't change topology, or remodel communication differently, but sometimes we've got to do what we've got to do (e.g. when systems are external to our organisation, or because relationships inside organisation, our team/module need to adapt).
